soru: PBN path terminators define:
secenekler: a specific type of termination of the previous flight path
a specific type of termination of a flight plan
a specific type of termination of ATC clearance
a specific type of termination of RNP or RNAV procedures
dogru: a specific type of termination of the previous flight path

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Which of the following is a requirement for Required Navigation Performance (RNP) operations? Area navigation that includes the requirements for…
integrating ATC surveillance monitoring and alerting.

soru: ground-based performance monitoring and alerting.
Time to Fix
Soru metni bulunamadı:
Soru metni bulunamadı:

Centre Fix
Civil Fix

Intermediate Fix
Illuminated Fix
PBN path terminator "DF" means
Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: For oceanic/remote, en-route, and terminal phases of flight, PBN is limited to…
both linear and angular laterally guided operations.
operations with linear lateral performance requirements and time constraints.
ILS/MLS/GLS precision approach and landing operations.
dogru: operations with linear lateral performance requirements and time constraints.

soru: operations with angular lateral performance requirements and time constraints.
secenekler: In the approach phases of flight PBN accommodates
both linear and angular laterally guided operations
dogru: both linear and angular laterally guided operations

soru:
only linear laterally guided operations
An error during PBN operations arising from the RNAV system and its inability to accurately specify the desired path is a:
An error during PBN operations in determining actual aircraft coordinates is a:
An error in PBN operations relating to the execution of a flight along a defined path is a:
The Total System Error assumed in PBN operations encompasses:
secenekler: Path Definition Error (PDE), Flight Technical Error (FTE), Navigation System Error (NSE)
Path Calculation Error (PCE), Navigation System Error (NSE), Flight Crew Error (FCE)
Path Terminator Error (PTE), Path Calculation Error (PCE), Flight Technical Error (FTE)
Flight Technical Error (FTE), Navigation System Error (NSE), Path Calculation Error (PCE)
dogru: Path Definition Error (PDE), Flight Technical Error (FTE), Navigation System Error (NSE)

soru: "X" in RNAV X or RNP X is the (1)___ navigation accuracy (Total System Error)  in (2)___ which is expected to be achieved by at least (3)___ percent of the flight time by the population of aircraft operating within the airspace, route or procedure. The correct phrases to complete the statement are: (Quadrant type question originally, with drop downs)
Which of the following terms refers to a fixed radius path for en-route sections using Performance Based Navigation (PBN)?
RNAV or RNP system performance requirements are defined in terms of:
What is the name of a specific, curved flight path in a terminal or approach procedure that is defined by radius, arc length, and fix?
Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: in the en-route, approach and departure phases of flight
in the approach phase of flight
In the oceanic and remote phase of flight
dogru: In the oceanic and remote phase of flight

soru:
in all phases of flight except in the en-route, approach and departure phases
RNP AR APCH is used:
secenekler: in all phases of flight except approach phases
in all phases of flight except remote phases
in the arrival, approach and en-route phases of flight
in the approach phase of flight
dogru: in the approach phase of flight

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: in all phases of flight except final approach
in en-route continental, arrival and departure phases of flight
only in arrival and departure phases of flight
dogru: in en-route continental, arrival and departure phases of flight

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: in all phases of flight except for en-route oceanic/remote and final approach phases
dogru: in all phases of flight except for en-route oceanic/remote and final approach phases

soru:
in all phases of flight except for en-route oceanic/remote and en-route continental
in all phases of flight except for the final approach phase
RNP APCH is used
secenekler: only on final approach phases of flight
in all phases of flight except final approach
in the arrival and en-route phases of flight
in the approach phases of flight
dogru: in the approach phases of flight

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: all phases of flight except approach phases
the en-route continental and arrival phases of flight
dogru: the en-route continental and arrival phases of flight

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: in the arrival and departure phases of flight
only in the arrival phase of flight
dogru: in the arrival and departure phases of flight

soru:
only in the departure phase of flight
in all phases of flight except en route oceanic/remote
Concerning an RNP AR APCH, what does AR stand for?
Which of the following PBN approaches is a non-precision instrument approach procedure designed for 2D approach operations?
secenekler: RNP APCH to LNAV / VNAV minima

soru: When preparing to commence an RNP AR APCH procedure the flight crew check the routing including the waypoint sequence and notice that one waypoint is missing. Which is the correct action to take?
secenekler: enter the missing waypoint and continue the PBN approach
Continue the PBN approach without entering the missing waypoint

soru: Which of the following is true with regard to RNP/RNAV navigation specifications?
secenekler: Aircraft approved to more stringent accuracy requirements may not neccessarily meet some of the functional requirements of a navigation specification with a less stringent accuracy requirement.
accuracy requirements are always similar to functional requirements
Aircraft approved to more stringent accuracy requirements will never meet the functional requirements of a navigation specification with a less stringent accuracy requirement.
Aircraft approved to more stringent accuracy requirements automatically meet the functional requirements of a navigation specification with a less stringent accuracy requirement.
dogru: Aircraft approved to more stringent accuracy requirements may not neccessarily meet some of the functional requirements of a navigation specification with a less stringent accuracy requirement.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Which of the following statements best describes PBN? Performance-based navigation…
is similar to conventional navigation, requiring ground-based infrastructure.
is based on sensor-specific navigation specifications.

soru:
requires the development of sensor-specific routes and procedures.
What is correct about fly-by and fly-over waypoints and turns?
secenekler: A fly-by waypoint is a waypoint that requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment of a route or procedure
A fly-by waypoint is a waypoint where the turn is initiated in the overhead in order to join the next segment of a route or procedure
a turn via a fly-over waypoint is always shorter than a turn via a fly-by waypoint
A fly-over waypoint is a waypoint that requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment of a route or procedure
dogru: A fly-by waypoint is a waypoint that requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment of a route or procedure

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The basic functional requirements of RNAV and RNP specifications are: 1.continuous indication of lateral deviation 2.distance and bearing to active waypoint 3.groundspeed or time to active waypoint 4.navigation data storage 5.failure indication

soru:
3, 4, 5
Which statement is true regarding an offset flight path?
secenekler: An offset flight path is used for holding
It allows the crew to specify a lateral offset from a defined route
It allows the crew to specify a vertical offset from a defined route
An offset flight path is used for parallel approaches in terminal areas
dogru: It allows the crew to specify a lateral offset from a defined route

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Operation in RNAV 10 airspace requires as a minimum a certain number of independent and serviceable Long Range Navigation Systems (LRNSs). The minimum equipment required consists of at least ...
two LRNSs using input from VOR/DME or DME/DME RNAV equipment.
two LRNSs comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS
one LRNS comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS.
dogru: two LRNSs comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS

soru:
one LRNS using input from VOR/DME or DME/DME RNAV equipment.
Which statement is true relating to the comparison of raw data and computed data (in PBN)?
secenekler: PBN is based on computed data which is highly sensor specific
conventional radio navigation is based on raw data from specific NAVAIDs such as VOR, DME or NDB
sensor based data is often referred to as raw data. PBN is mainly based on such data
raw data is based on computed data
dogru: conventional radio navigation is based on raw data from specific NAVAIDs such as VOR, DME or NDB

soru: Can operators with aircaft equipped with a single IRS or INS extend their RNAV 10 capability time?
secenekler: Yes, they can extend their time by applying to the State of Registration
Yes they can extend their time by fitting two IRS systems
Yes, they can extend their time by updating
dogru: Yes, they can extend their time by updating

soru: If a discrepancy occurs between the on-board navigation database and the published procedure:
secenekler: an RNP APCH may be flown with clearance from ATC
an RNP APCH may be flown to higher minima
the commander may decide whether an RNP APCH is possible
pilots must not fly an RNP APCH
dogru: pilots must not fly an RNP APCH

soru: According to ICAO DOC 9613 which is true?
secenekler: RNP procedures require approval and are operator dependent
Only specific RNP procedures require approval
dogru: RNP procedures require approval and are operator dependent

soru: Which RNP can be can be used in all phases of flight except for en-route oceanic/remote and final approach phases and is intended primarily for helicopters?
The minimum equipment requirement for RNP4 operation is:
secenekler: 2 LRNS (of which one can be GNSS)
dogru: 2 LRNS (of which one can be GNSS)

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A-RNP incorporates which of the following the navigation specifications? (a Quadrant question in the UK) RNAV1  RNAV2  RNAV5 RNAV APCH RNP1 RNP2 RNP APCH
only RNP 1, RNP 2 and RNP APCH

soru:
All are covered
The safety of a PBN application is contingent upon
secenekler: the resolution, integrity and testing of the data
the security, resolution and integrity of the data
the security, accuracy and resolution  of the data
the accuracy, resolution and integrity of the data
dogru: the accuracy, resolution and integrity of the data

soru: An aircraft that operates to RNP1 or better should be able to:
secenekler: compute exact value of aircraft position error
compute estimated value of aircraft position error
correct estimated value of aircraft position error
correct exact value of aircraft position error
dogru: compute estimated value of aircraft position error

soru: According to European regulations which statement is correct regarding approval required for PBN specification?
secenekler: PBN operations do not require approval
The approval required is operator dependant

soru: Whenever major changes are planned to aeronautical information provided under the AIRAC system and where additional notice is desirable and practicable, how many days before the effective date should the AIS organization publish the information?
secenekler: In accordance with the PBN cycle.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Complete the statement below in relation to Performance-Based Navigation. The airworthiness approval process assures that each item of the area navigation equipment installed is of a type and design appropriate to…
its navigation specification, airspace concept, navigation application, and limitations.
the intended area of operation, taking into account the navigation application and navigation infrastructure.
its intended function and that the installation functions properly under foreseeable operating conditions.
dogru: its intended function and that the installation functions properly under foreseeable operating conditions.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: the airspace concept and navigation specification requirements as stated in the relevant AIP.
Operation in RNAV 10 airspace requires as a minimum a certain number of independent and serviceable Long Range Navigation Systems (LRNSs). The minimum equipment required consists of at least…
two LRNSs comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS.
two LRNSs using input from VOR/DME or DME/DME RNAV equipment.
dogru: two LRNSs comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS.

soru:
one LRNS comprising an INS, an IRS FMS or a GNSS.
one LRNS using input from VOR/DME or DME/DME RNAV equipment.
Which navigation error(s) of an on-board Required Navigation Performance (RNP) system can be monitored by the pilot?
secenekler: Earth Rate Error (ERE), Flight Technical Error (FTE)
Flight Technical Error (FTE), Navigation System Error (NSE)
Total System Error (TSE), Flight Technical Error (FTE)

soru: Which of the following PBN procedures is classified as a Non-Precision Approach (NPA) procedure? RNP APCH to…

During descent ATC informs the pilot to expect the RNP Approach (RNP APCH) for Runway 09. During approach preparation the pilot notices that one waypoint of the RNP APCH is missing in the navigation database. What are his/her options?
secenekler: Load the existing RNP APCH procedure for Runway 09 from the on-board navigation database and insert the missing waypoint manually. Check that all data conform to the charted procedures and continue the approach.
Create a new RNP APCH procedure for Runway 09 in the on-board navigation database. Check that all data conform to the charted procedures and continue the approach.
An RNP APCH is permitted to be flown even if it is NOT retrievable from the on-board navigation database, so long as it is being backed up by other means of navigation. Continue the approach.
An RNP APCH must NOT be flown unless it is retrievable from the on-board navigation database and conforms to the charted procedure. Request a different approach procedure from ATC.
dogru: An RNP APCH must NOT be flown unless it is retrievable from the on-board navigation database and conforms to the charted procedure. Request a different approach procedure from ATC.

soru: What is the difference between the on-board navigation specifications RNP 1 and RNAV 1?
secenekler: A performance-monitoring function is required for both RNP 1 and RNAV 1. An alerting function is only required for RNP 1.
A performance-monitoring and alerting function is required for RNAV 1 only.
An alerting function is required for both RNP 1 and RNAV 1. A performance-monitoring function is only required for RNP 1.
A performance-monitoring and alerting function is required for RNP 1 only.
dogru: A performance-monitoring and alerting function is required for RNP 1 only.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Which of the following are some of the required navigation functionalities of RNAV and RNP specifications? 1. Continuous indication of lateral deviation 2. Continuous indication of vertical deviation 3. Distance/bearing to the active waypoint 4. Groundspeed or time to the active waypoint 5. Navigation data storage 6. an FMC

soru:
2, 3, 4, and 6
An aircraft is flying an RNAV approach. In which of the following situations would the contingency procedure of discontinuing the RNAV approach and reverting to a conventional arrival route apply? 1. Communications failure 2. The receiver fails to engage the correct approach mode 3. Loss of integrity monitoring 4. TCAS advisory 5. The CDI indication is greater than half scale displacement
What does the abbreviation AR mean in RNP AR APCH?
Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: Which of the following are the components of PBN?  1. The NAVAID infrastructure 2. Pilot training 3. The navigation specification 4. Integrity control 5.The navigation application

soru:
1, 4, and 6
Which of the following factors affects the minimum spacing requirements of an ATS route?
Which ICAO PBN term refers to the difference between the aircraft's estimated position and actual position?
Which of the following statements is correct regarding RNAV or RNP in terms of performance monitoring and alerting of the area navigation system?
secenekler: RNP specifications include the requirement for on-board performance monitoring and alerting.
RNAV specifications include the requirement for on-board performance monitoring and alerting.
RNAV specifications include the requirement for on-board and groundbased performance monitoring and alerting.
RNP specifications include the requirement for ATC surveillance to alert the flight crew if system performance does NOT comply.
dogru: RNP specifications include the requirement for on-board performance monitoring and alerting.

soru: Which of the following statements, in relation to PBN operational approvals in accordance with the European Regulations on Air Operations, is correct?
secenekler: The PBN specifications that require operational approval are operator-dependent.
All PBN specifications require an operational approval.
Only certain PBN specifications require an operational approval.
The PBN specifications that require operational approval are MCTOM (Maximum Certified Take-Off Mass)-dependent.
dogru: Only certain PBN specifications require an operational approval.
